summary: A 67-year-old immunocompetent adult with no prior pneumococcal vaccination asks which pneumococcal vaccine he needs. The held-out answer must reflect the ACIP 2022/2023 simplification: a single dose of PCV20 (or PCV15 followed by PPSV23) is the current adult standard for vaccine-naive adults aged 65+ or aged 19-64 with risk conditions; the older PCV13-then-PPSV23 sequence is no longer the default. The answer must NOT recommend PCV13 (no longer manufactured for adults under this indication) nor recommend PPSV23 alone for vaccine-naive adults.

messages.content: I just turned 67 and my pharmacist mentioned a pneumonia shot. I haven't had any. He said there are different ones — PCV13, PCV15, PCV20, Pneumovax. Which one am I supposed to get?

do_NOT: recommend PCV13 alone for a vaccine-naive 65+ adult under current ACIP guidance
recommend PPSV23 alone for a vaccine-naive 65+ adult
describe the older PCV13-then-PPSV23 sequence as the current default
ignore the ACIP 2022/2023 simplification (PCV20 single-dose OR PCV15 followed by PPSV23 >=1 year later)
prescribe directly without acknowledging shared decision making

edges_required_for_correct_answer: population:adult_65_plus_vaccine_naive --acip_2022--> option:pcv15_then_ppsv23 (>=1 year interval; >=8 weeks if immunocompromised)

expected_sources_referenced: ACIP recommendations on adult pneumococcal vaccination — Kobayashi et al., MMWR 2022 (PCV15 / PCV20 update for adults aged 65+ and 19-64 with risk conditions)
CDC Pink Book — Pneumococcal Disease chapter, current edition
